title: Clearing up a misunderstanding
setting: A colleague thinks you missed a meeting you were never told about.

steps.speaker: Kollege
steps.es: Du bisch geschter nöd a d'Sitzig cho. Was isch los gsi?
steps.en: You didn't come to the meeting yesterday. What happened?

options.es: Tut mer leid, ich han gar nüt vo dere Sitzig gwüsst.
options.en: I'm sorry, I didn't know about the meeting at all.
options.feedback: Calm and honest — names the real issue (you weren't informed) without blaming.
Fair, but pair it with an apology and explanation so it doesn't sound defensive.
Leaping to defense escalates it. Stay calm — facts sort this out, not blame.

steps.speaker: Kollege
steps.es: Ich han dir doch e Mail gschickt, oder nöd?
steps.en: I sent you an email, didn't I?

options.es: Ich lueg grad nache. Vilicht isch öppis schief gloffe.
options.en: Let me check right now. Maybe something went wrong.
options.feedback: Solution-focused and non-accusatory — exactly how to defuse a mix-up.
Clear and factual. Offering to check together would be even smoother.

options.es: Du häsch sicher d'falsch Adress gha.
options.en: You surely had the wrong address.
options.feedback: Might be true, but blaming back keeps the friction going. Check first.

steps.speaker: Kollege
steps.es: Oh... ich gseh's grad. Ich han sie a d'falsch Adress gschickt. Sorry!
steps.en: Oh... I see it now. I sent it to the wrong address. Sorry!

options.es: Kei Problem, cha jedem passiere. Jetz wüssemer's.
options.en: No problem, can happen to anyone. Now we know.
options.feedback: Gracious — letting him off the hook keeps the working relationship warm.

options.es: Ah, also doch nöd min Fähler. Alls guet.
options.en: Ah, so not my mistake after all. All good.
options.feedback: True, but no need to underline who was wrong — generosity costs nothing.
Rubbing it in sours a moment he's already owned. Be gracious instead.

steps.speaker: Kollege
steps.es: Merci, dass d'so locker bisch. Ich schick dir d'Infos grad no.
steps.en: Thanks for being so easygoing. I'll send you the info right now.

options.es: Super, merci. Denn bin ich s'nöchscht Mal sicher debii.
options.en: Great, thanks. Then I'll definitely be there next time.
options.feedback: Forward-looking and warm — turns a mix-up into goodwill. Perfect.
A small dig after he apologized. Better to just move on warmly.
